seoTitle: How to Delete or Hide a Conversation on Slack
metaDescription: Delete individual Slack messages, hide a direct message thread from your sidebar, or archive a channel to keep your workspace tidy.
intro: You can't fully erase a shared direct message history for both parties without admin access, but you can clean up your own view. This guide covers deleting individual messages, hiding a DM thread, and archiving old channels.

steps.heading: Select a message to delete
steps.body: Hover over the target message in an active conversation to reveal the message options.

steps.heading: Delete the message
steps.body: Click the three-dot menu, choose 'Delete message', and confirm the warning prompt to permanently remove it.

steps.heading: Hide a direct message thread
steps.body: In the left sidebar, hover over a DM entry and click the close (X) icon to remove it from view without deleting the history.

steps.heading: Archive a channel
steps.body: Open a project channel, access its options, and select Archive to freeze the conversation stream.

steps.heading: Set automatic message expiration
steps.body: In workspace settings, configure a retention policy to automatically clear older messages after a set period.

faq.question: Can I delete a message someone else sent?
faq.answer: Only workspace admins can delete other members' messages; regular users can only delete their own.

faq.question: Does hiding a DM delete the conversation history?
faq.answer: No, hiding just removes it from your sidebar; the history reappears if that contact messages you again.

faq.question: Is there a time limit for deleting a message?
faq.answer: Slack generally allows deleting your own messages at any time, though workspace policies may restrict this.

faq.question: Can I recover a deleted message?
faq.answer: Deleted messages typically cannot be recovered by regular users once removed.
